scope: source-model inventory of the bounded local mounted-userspace comparator envelope for storage.local.successor_comparator.v1

device_media_assumptions: Evidence may describe only explicit local byte-addressable devices admitted to a TideFS pool by current source-controlled flows.
Evidence must record whether the device is loop-backed, virtual, solid-state, rotational, or otherwise constrained by the runner or harness.
Evidence must not transfer a result between media classes without a new artifact row that names the media and measurement source.
mounted_local_filesystem_mode: Comparable rows are limited to a single-node mounted local filesystem path.
Rows must identify whether execution used FUSE, mounted kernel VFS, or another source-owned local path.
Rows do not cover local block-volume export unless a separate registered claim names that mode.
workload_classes: metadata create, rename, unlink, and lookup rows may be compared only when the operation set and durability boundary are named
read, write, flush, fsync, fdatasync, statfs, xattr, lock, and mmap/writeback rows may be compared only when the mounted tier and completion semantics are named
failure rows may be compared only when the crash, restart, media, import, or recovery injection point is named
measurement_attribution: Each row must attribute observed latency, throughput, correctness, or refusal behavior to a concrete artifact, command, workflow run, or source-model review.
Harness setup, runner media, mount tier, and current source ref must be part of the row.
A row label alone is not evidence for the claim.
fault_model: The local envelope can cover process restart, FUSE daemon exit, local mount teardown, explicit device refusal, source-model recovery review, or runner-scoped media fault injection when the artifact names the injection point.
The local envelope does not cover multi-node quorum, RDMA, cluster membership, remote replication, geo-RPO, or production availability.
non_comparable_cases: OpenZFS, DRBD, ext4, XFS, or local filesystem results from external publications are not comparable without matching workload, media, mount tier, durability boundary, and measurement method.
Distributed storage, clustered storage, block export, release-candidate, and production-readiness claims are outside this artifact.
Ambition statements, design documents, issue labels, and closed historical issue numbers do not make a local comparator row comparable.

residual_risk: This artifact defines the envelope only; it does not provide incumbent measurements.
Mounted-userspace rows still need source-qualified runtime evidence before they can support any local comparator-performance text.
The registry claim remains blocked and the umbrella storage-intent successor/comparator boundary is outside this artifact.
